question: You have initialized a local directory and need to link it to a remote GitHub repository named origin. What command connects the local repository to the remote URL?
You have accidentally staged a file named 'config.js' but want to remove it from the staging area without deleting the file from your disk. What command do you use?
You want to see a summary of changes in your working directory, specifically seeing which files are modified versus untracked. What command provides this status?
You need to create a new branch named 'feature-auth' and switch to it immediately. What is the single command to accomplish this?
You have made changes to multiple files and want to stage them all for commit using a single command. What command do you run?
You are on the 'main' branch and want to incorporate changes from 'feature-auth' into your current branch. What command merges the branches?
You need to view the commit history including the author, date, and commit message for the last 5 commits. What command do you use?
You want to discard all local changes in a specific file and revert it to the state of the last commit. What command do you run?
After pushing a new branch to GitHub for the first time, you want to set the upstream tracking so you can just type 'git push' later. What command do you use?
You need to verify the URL of the remote repository currently configured for your project. What command displays this information?
How do you perform a 'git rebase' to maintain a linear project history when pulling changes from a remote repository?
You need to list all branches in your local repository and see which one is currently active. What command do you use?
If you want to view the differences between your working directory and the last commit for a specific file, what command do you use?
You need to change the commit message of your last commit before pushing it to the remote server. What command do you use?
What is the specific command to delete a local branch named 'old-feature' after it has been merged?
What command is used in Git to temporarily save changes in a dirty working directory so you can switch branches, and later reapply those changes?
Which Git command is used to display the 'reflog', allowing you to recover commits that are no longer reachable by any branch or tag?
How do you force a push to a remote repository, overwriting the remote history with your local history?
What command allows you to cherry-pick a specific commit from another branch and apply it to your current active branch?
If you need to remove a file from both the Git index (staging area) and the file system, what command do you execute?
